title: make_store() names temp file _f, implying unused — but it is load-bearing RAII
description: In wallet/src/db.rs tests, make_store() returns (WalletStore, NamedTempFile). Callers write let (store, _f) = make_store().await. Rust convention: _ prefix means intentionally unused. Here _f is kept alive intentionally — dropping it would delete the temp file mid-test. A future contributor doing 'cleanup' might change it to let (store, _) = make_store().await which drops the NamedTempFile immediately, causing tests to fail spuriously. Fix: rename to _tempfile or add a comment in make_store explaining the lifetime contract.
